name: embody-codec
documentation:
# EmBody protocol codec

[![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/embody-codec.svg)][pypi_]
[![Status](https://img.shields.io/pypi/status/embody-codec.svg)][status]
[![Python Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/embody-codec)][python version]
[![License](https://img.shields.io/pypi/l/embody-codec)][license]

[![Tests](https://github.com/aidee-health/embody-codec/workflows/Tests/badge.svg)][tests]

[![pre-commit](https://img.shields.io/badge/pre--commit-enabled-brightgreen?logo=pre-commit&logoColor=white)][pre-commit]
[![Black](https://img.shields.io/badge/code%20style-black-000000.svg)][black]

[pypi_]: https://pypi.org/project/embody-codec/
[status]: https://pypi.org/project/embody-codec/
[python version]: https://pypi.org/project/embody-codec
[tests]: https://github.com/aidee-health/embody-codec/actions?workflow=Tests
[pre-commit]: https://github.com/pre-commit/pre-commit
[black]: https://github.com/psf/black

This is a Python based implementation library for the Aidee EmBody communication protocol.

## Features

- Encode protocol messages to binary data (bytes)
- Decode binary data to protocol messages
- Accessory code

## Requirements

- This library does not require any external libraries
- Requires Python 3.8+

# Installing package with pip from github

You can install _embody codec_ via [pip] from [PyPI]:

```console
pip install embody-codec
```

## Contributing

modules.name: embodycodec.file_codec
documentation:
File Codec for the Aidee EmBody device.

This class separates out the parsing of different message types from the EmBody device's file format. The first part
consists of all the different messages wrapped as subclasses of the ProtocolMessage dataclass. The bottom part
provides access methods for parsing one and one message from a bytes object.

functions.name: embodycodec.file_codec.decode_message
documentation:
Decodes a bytes object into proper subclass of ProtocolMessage.

raises LookupError if unknown message type.

modules.name: embodycodec.attributes
documentation:
Attribute types for the EmBody device

All attribute types inherits from the Attribute class, and provides self-contained encoding and decoding of
attributes.

functions.name: embodycodec.attributes.decode_executive_command_response
documentation:
Decodes a bytes object into proper attribute object.

Raises BufferError if data buffer is too short. Returns None if unknown attribute
Raises LookupError if unknown message type.

functions.name: embodycodec.attributes.decode_attribute
documentation:
Decodes a bytes object into proper attribute object.

Raises BufferError if data buffer is too short.
Raises LookupError if unknown message type.

classes.name: embodycodec.attributes.Attribute

class_variables.name: embodycodec.attributes.Attribute.struct_format
documentation: struct format used to pack/unpack object - must be set by subclasses

class_variables.name: embodycodec.attributes.Attribute.attribute_id
documentation: attribute id field - must be set by subclasses

class_variables.name: embodycodec.attributes.Attribute.value
documentation: value is implemented and overridden by subclasses.
Abstract base class for attribute types

classes.name: embodycodec.attributes.ZeroTerminatedStringAttribute
documentation: Zero terminated string is actually not zero terminated - only length terminated...

methods.name: embodycodec.attributes.AfeSettingsAllAttribute.decode
documentation: Special handling. certain versions of the device returns an empty attribute value.

modules.name: embodycodec.codec
documentation:
Codec for the EmBody device
A full embodycodec for the protocol specified for the EmBody device

All protocol message types inherits from the Message class, and provides self-contained encoding and decoding of
messages.

functions.name: embodycodec.codec.decode
documentation:
Decodes a bytes object into proper message object.

raises BufferError if data buffer is too short.
raises DecodeError if error decoding message.
raises LookupError if unknown message type.
raises CrcError if checksum fails.

classes.name: embodycodec.codec.Message

methods.name: embodycodec.codec.Message.decode
documentation: Decode bytes into message object

methods.name: embodycodec.codec.Message.encode
documentation: Encode a message object to bytes
Note: Default method encodes dataclass with fixed length and format

class_variables.name: embodycodec.codec.Message.hdr_len
documentation: Header length to avoid checking every time

class_variables.name: embodycodec.codec.Message.crc_len
documentation: CRC length to avoid magic numbers

class_variables.name: embodycodec.codec.Message.struct_hdr_format
documentation: unpack format that is fixed for all headers, taking up hdr_len bytes

class_variables.name: embodycodec.codec.Message.struct_format
documentation:
unpack format to be overridden by sub-classes, see
https://docs.python.org/3/library/struct.html#format-characters
does not include header (type and length field) or footer (crc)

class_variables.name: embodycodec.codec.Message.msg_type
documentation: Protocol type field - must be set by subclasses

class_variables.name: embodycodec.codec.Message.crc
documentation: crc footer is dynamically set

class_variables.name: embodycodec.codec.Message.length
documentation: Length of entire message (header + body + crc). length is dynamically set
Abstract base class for protocol messages

class_variables.name: embodycodec.codec.FileDataChunk.struct_format
documentation: Format of new package uses little endian inside for maximum speed of transfer at device side

class_variables.name: embodycodec.codec.FileDataChunk.fileref
documentation: Used to identify to the host which file the chunk belongs to. Taken from the send command as supplied by host
